utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. Now check the final coverage plan: it includes all five categories. For health_coverage, both physical and mental health insurances are specified with costs and plan names. Property_coverage includes home/contents insurance with cost and plan details. Travel_coverage includes travel insurance with cost and plan details. Additional_services are included with cost and plan descriptions. Total_budget includes both monthly and annual cost breakdowns. Each item is fully specified according to what should be covered under each item, with all required details present.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The AI assistant's final plan includes several insurance packages. To map them to ratings, I look for each named plan in the ground-truth data. For physical health insurance, the assistant selected 'LifeFirst Standard Package90' (interpreted as 'LifeFirst Standard Plan', rating 8) and 'EliteCare Premium Physical Health Plan9' (interpreted as 'EliteCare Premium Plan', rating 10). For mental health insurance, it selected 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage38', which best matches 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' under mental health, rating 6. For property coverage, 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage12' matches 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage' under home content, rating 7. For travel insurance, 'InsurePlus Standard Coverage11' aligns with 'InsurePlus Standard Plan' under travel, rating 7, and 'EliteTravel Premium Plan1' aligns with 'EliteTravel Premium Plan', rating 10. Finally, under additional services, the assistant selected 'LifeFirst Standard Package19' (again 'LifeFirst Standard Plan', rating 8) and 'Employer's Elite Expat Health & Travel Plan' (not found in the ground-truth ratings, so no rating can be assigned).
